task: I need to write a simple note for the front desk about a room that got damaged during a stay, but I want it to sound clear and professional without getting too fancy. Can you help me draft something that covers what happened, what needs to be passed along, and who should see it next?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, professional note you can use:

**Front Desk Note:**  
Please be advised that the room was damaged during the guest’s stay. Kindly document the issue and pass this information along to the appropriate department for follow-up. Please ensure the next shift and management are made aware.

If you want, I can also make it sound **more formal**, **more casual**, or tailor it for a **specific hotel/property style**.

user_simulator.text: Close enough, but a little too generic. I need it to say what got damaged, that it happened during the stay, and who exactly should get it next — front desk, housekeeping, maintenance, maybe manager. Keep it plain, not wordy. Something I can actually drop in the log.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a plain, log-ready version:

**Front Desk Note:**  
Room damage was found during the guest’s stay. The [item/area] was damaged and needs to be reported to front desk, housekeeping, maintenance, and the manager for follow-up.

If you want, I can also turn it into a **one-line incident log entry** with placeholders you can fill in quickly.

user_simulator.text: Better, but still too vague. Put the actual damaged thing in the note, not just [item/area], and make it read like a real log entry. Something like: room damage found during stay — [bed frame / lamp / carpet / etc.] damaged. Notify front desk, housekeeping, maintenance, and manager for follow-up. Plain and short.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a plain, short log-style version:

**Room damage found during stay — [bed frame / lamp / carpet / etc.] damaged. Notify front desk, housekeeping, maintenance, and manager for follow-up.**

If you want, I can also make it even more like a **real incident log entry** with a date/time line and room number.

user_simulator.text: Better. Still need it a little more like a real log entry though — with the room number and maybe a spot for date/time. Keep the same plain tone. Something I can just fill in and use.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a simple fill-in version you can drop into the log:

**Date/Time:** [MM/DD/YYYY, HH:MM]  
**Room:** [Room Number]  
**Room damage found during stay — [damaged item] damaged. Notify front desk, housekeeping, maintenance, and manager for follow-up.**

If you want, I can make it even more compact into a single-line log entry.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Plain and usable. I can fill in the date, room, and what got damaged without messing with it.
